body.title: [akien] Migrate UC TLS to real domain theigors.dev
body.description:
Replace mkcert (localhost-only) with real domain theigors.dev for UC TLS. Steps: (1) register theigors.dev (~$12/yr Cloudflare/Porkbun), (2) /etc/hosts entry pointing to 127.0.0.1, (3) Let's Encrypt DNS-01 ACME challenge via lego or certbot+registrar plugin to issue cert valid for theigors.dev + *.theigors.dev, (4) swap igor.cfg IGOR_SSL_CERT/KEY paths, (5) auto-renewal cron. mkcert bootstrap stays as fallback. Goal: zero-warning HTTPS for all UC clients without local CA install.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/.agent_datacenter/Igor-wild-0001/igor.cfg (IGOR_SSL_CERT/KEY paths); a new lego/certbot config (DNS-01 ACME challenge); /etc/hosts (127.0.0.1 → theigors.dev); lab/utility_closet/installer/cert_setup.sh (or wherever cert bootstrap lives).

**Design rules:** no-secrets-in-repo (creds for registrar API stay in .env); docs-in-code (cert_setup.sh top-of-file documents the steps).

**Scope boundary:** IN — domain registration + DNS-01 + cert issuance + igor.cfg path swap + auto-renewal cron. OUT — multi-tenant cert management; non-Tailscale tailnets (separate T-tailscale-cert-restore).

**Test plan:** smoke test: `curl https://theigors.dev:<UC port>` returns 200 with no cert warning; renewal cron dry-run succeeds. mkcert fallback path verified by env override.
